ambiguities.input_contextualized_conventional_defaul: 'on account' = accounts payable — merchandise purchase creates a liability
'freight to warehouse' = inbound freight necessary to bring inventory to its present location and condition, capitalized to inventory cost
ambiguities.input_contextualized_ifrs_default: IAS 2.11 requires costs of purchase include freight and handling costs necessary to bring inventories to their present location and condition

ambiguities.input_contextualized_conventional_defaul: 'shipping insurance' in context of inbound freight = cost necessary to bring inventory to present location, capitalized to inventory
ambiguities.input_contextualized_ifrs_default: IAS 2.11 includes insurance costs incurred in bringing inventories to their present location as part of inventory cost

ambiguities.input_contextualized_conventional_defaul: 'post-acquisition' storage = storage after inventory arrived, not necessary to bring inventory to saleable condition
ambiguities.input_contextualized_ifrs_default: IAS 2.16 explicitly excludes storage costs from inventory cost unless necessary in the production process before a further production stage

ambiguities.input_contextualized_conventional_defaul: 'outbound delivery' = freight to customers, not necessary to bring inventory to warehouse location
ambiguities.input_contextualized_ifrs_default: IAS 2.16 excludes selling costs from inventory cost; outbound freight is a distribution/selling cost

best_attempt.reason: Merchandise $1,000,000 + freight to warehouse $120,000 + shipping insurance $30,000 capitalized to inventory per IAS 2.11; post-acquisition storage $20,000 and outbound freight $50,000 expensed per IAS 2.16; accounts payable for merchandise, cash for all other costs
